session_id: ANL-2026-04-25-规则与-jest-静态融合契约测试
topic: 规则与 Jest 的静态融合：契约式测试

relevant_files.summary: Computes rule metadata from .fabric/bootstrap and .fabric/agents, including file hash, stable_id, identity_source, and revision.
Builds Fabric health reports and is structured as composable checks such as meta revision, protected paths, ledger, and audit.
Defines strict rule metadata schema; preserving it for rule selection argues for a sidecar rule-test index.
Documents contract-first Jest categories: logic firewall, runtime contract, and shell regression.
Enforces runtime contract cleanup by checking active timers, listener leaks, and scene residue.

patterns.description: sync-meta computes hashes and stable identities and writes generated Fabric metadata.
doctor reduces individual checks into a single status and can add rule-test staleness as another check.
Project tests drive lifecycle through public contract helpers and assert zero residue.

constraints: Do not generate Jest test code dynamically.
Do not encode rule text or rule predicates into Jest helpers.
Keep rule selection metadata lean and stable.
Static checks must be fast enough for doctor and pre-commit style usage.

integration_points.description: Scan test files and write .fabric/rule-test.index.json after computing AgentsMeta.
Read AgentsMeta and rule-test index; warn when linked rule hash changed while linked test hash did not.
Add a strict schema for rule-test index shape.
Document contract testing as static traceability, not executable rule logic.

key_findings: sync-meta already computes the exact rule hashes needed for staleness detection.
doctor already reports metadata drift and audit violations, so rule-test staleness fits its existing model.
werewolf-minigame already has a mature contract-first Jest vocabulary that can be referenced instead of reinvented.
A sidecar index is preferable to extending AgentsMetaNode because tests are verification metadata, not rule selection metadata.
